slides.title: School and Learning
bullets: Prepare to read, discuss and write
slides.narration: Welcome to Grade 3 English, Unit 2: School and Learning. Welcome to Unit 2! This unit is all about school and learning. You will talk about your lessons, your teacher, and the subjects you study, like mathematics and grammar. You will learn the words for the supplies you use every day, such as an eraser and your books. You will find out how to get ready for a contest, write a short report, and use the library well. Best of all, you will learn to talk about things that happened in the past — what you studied, where you went, and why you enjoyed it. By the end, you will be ready to cheer for the Grammar Champions in this unit's story! In this short lecture, preview the key ideas and prepare for the independent lesson.

slides.title: What you will achieve
bullets: Name the people, places, and supplies at school, using words like teacher, library, and eraser
Talk about your school subjects, such as mathematics and grammar
Tell about things that happened before, using the simple past tense (studied, prepared, went)
slides.narration: By the end of this unit, you will be able to name the people, places, and supplies at school, using words like teacher, library, and eraser. you will also talk about your school subjects, such as mathematics and grammar. you will also tell about things that happened before, using the simple past tense (studied, prepared, went). Keep these goals in mind as you work.

slides.title: Language for precise thinking
slides.narration: Your vocabulary work begins with these words: teacher, author, library, graduate, mathematics, grammar. Listen to each pronunciation in the vocabulary lab, study its meaning in context, and use the word accurately in your own discussion and writing.

slides.title: Read critically
slides.narration: The reading sequence includes A Day at School and Getting Ready for the Contest. Read actively. Identify each text's central ideas, notice viewpoint and reliability, and support every inference with relevant evidence from the text.

slides.title: The Simple Past Tense
bullets: We use the simple past tense to talk about things that already happened — yesterday, last week, or a long time ago. It helps us tell stories and say what...
slides.narration: The first language focus is The Simple Past Tense. We use the simple past tense to talk about things that already happened — yesterday, last week, or a long time ago. It helps us tell stories and say what we did. Notice the structure in the model examples, then apply it deliberately in connected sentences and paragraphs.

slides.title: Use English for a real purpose
bullets: Speaking 1 — Talk About Your School Day
Explain, support and improve your ideas
slides.narration: In speaking, you will complete Speaking 1 — Talk About Your School Day. Organise your ideas, use evidence and respond clearly to questions. In writing, you will plan, draft, check and revise. Use the success criteria before submitting your work.

slides.title: You are ready to begin
slides.narration: You are ready to begin. Start with the vocabulary lab, continue through reading and comprehension, apply the grammar focus, and complete the speaking and writing practices. Use AI English for hints and feedback, and bring your questions to the live sessions.
